title: chamaemelum flahaultii
intro: Chamaemelum flahaultii, also known as Flahault's chamomile or lesser chamomile, is a low-growing, perennial plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a member of the same genus as the more commonly known German chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla), but has distinct properties and uses. Chamaemelum flahaultii is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which make it useful for soothing digestive issues and promoting relaxation. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its health benefits and provide relief from various health concerns.
